In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ}} Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:Sayama Shrine]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}

{{еҮәе…ёгҒ®жҳҺиЁҳ|date=2018-09-09}} {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ}} |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|29|41.2|N|135|33|25.1|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ}} Handa 1-223 |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34 |з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 29 |з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 41.2 | N(еҢ—з·Ҝ)еҸҠгҒіS(еҚ—з·Ҝ) = N |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135 |зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 33 |зөҢеәҰз§’ = 25.1 | E(жқұзөҢ)еҸҠгҒіW(иҘҝзөҢ) = E | ең°еӣіеӣҪгӮігғјгғү = JP |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|дҪҸеҗүйҖ }} |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ }} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}}, and its former ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң{{ill|Ushigashira TennЕҚ|ja|зүӣй ӯеӨ©зҺӢ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ}} Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ}} Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Sayama Shrine]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}

{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|дҪҸеҗүйҖ }} |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ }} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}}, and its former ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң{{ill|Ushigashira TennЕҚ|ja|зүӣй ӯеӨ©зҺӢ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ}} Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ}} Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:Sayama Shrine]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}}, and its former ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң{{ill|Ushigashira TennЕҚ|ja|зүӣй ӯеӨ©зҺӢ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ}} Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ}}) * It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}}, and its former ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң{{ill|Ushigashira TennЕҚ|ja|зүӣй ӯеӨ©зҺӢ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ}} Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ}} Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:Sayama Shrine]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sayama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

{{еҮәе…ёгҒ®жҳҺиЁҳ|date=2018-09-09}} {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|29|41.2|N|135|33|25.1|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ|12=draft}} Handa 1-223 |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34 |з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 29 |з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 41.2 | N(еҢ—з·Ҝ)еҸҠгҒіS(еҚ—з·Ҝ) = N |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135 |зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 33 |зөҢеәҰз§’ = 25.1 | E(жқұзөҢ)еҸҠгҒіW(иҘҝзөҢ) = E | ең°еӣіеӣҪгӮігғјгғү = JP |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|дҪҸеҗүйҖ |12=draft}} |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |12=draft}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}}, and its former ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|12=draft}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|12=draft}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|12=draft}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң{{ill|Ushigashira TennЕҚ|ja|зүӣй ӯеӨ©зҺӢ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|12=draft}} Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң|12=draft}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|12=draft}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ|12=draft}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ|12=draft}} Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ|12=draft}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі|12=draft}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Sayama Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}

The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Sayama Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}

{{еҮәе…ёгҒ®жҳҺиЁҳ|date=2018-09-09}} {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|29|41.2|N|135|33|25.1|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ|12=draft}} Handa 1-223 |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34 |з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 29 |з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 41.2 | N(еҢ—з·Ҝ)еҸҠгҒіS(еҚ—з·Ҝ) = N |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135 |зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 33 |зөҢеәҰз§’ = 25.1 | E(жқұзөҢ)еҸҠгҒіW(иҘҝзөҢ) = E | ең°еӣіеӣҪгӮігғјгғү = JP |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|дҪҸеҗүйҖ |12=draft}} |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |12=draft}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}}, and its former ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|12=draft}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|12=draft}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|12=draft}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң{{ill|Ushigashira TennЕҚ|ja|зүӣй ӯеӨ©зҺӢ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|12=draft}} Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң|12=draft}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|12=draft}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ|12=draft}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ|12=draft}} Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ|12=draft}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі|12=draft}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Sayama Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}

The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|дҪҸеҗүйҖ |12=draft}} |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |12=draft}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}}, and its former ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|12=draft}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|12=draft}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|12=draft}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң{{ill|Ushigashira TennЕҚ|ja|зүӣй ӯеӨ©зҺӢ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|12=draft}} Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң|12=draft}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ|12=draft}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі|12=draft}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{еҮәе…ёгҒ®жҳҺиЁҳ|date=2018-09-09}} {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|29|41.2|N|135|33|25.1|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ|12=draft}} Handa 1-223 |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34 |з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 29 |з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 41.2 | N(еҢ—з·Ҝ)еҸҠгҒіS(еҚ—з·Ҝ) = N |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135 |зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 33 |зөҢеәҰз§’ = 25.1 | E(жқұзөҢ)еҸҠгҒіW(иҘҝзөҢ) = E | ең°еӣіеӣҪгӮігғјгғү = JP |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|дҪҸеҗүйҖ |12=draft}} |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |12=draft}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}}, and its former ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|12=draft}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|12=draft}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|12=draft}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң{{ill|Ushigashira TennЕҚ|ja|зүӣй ӯеӨ©зҺӢ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|12=draft}} Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң|12=draft}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|12=draft}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ|12=draft}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ|12=draft}} Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ|12=draft}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі|12=draft}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}

{{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ|12=draft}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ|12=draft}} Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ|12=draft}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі|12=draft}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{еҮәе…ёгҒ®жҳҺиЁҳ|date=2018-09-09}} {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|29|41.2|N|135|33|25.1|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft}} Handa 1-223 |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34 |з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 29 |з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 41.2 | N(еҢ—з·Ҝ)еҸҠгҒіS(еҚ—з·Ҝ) = N |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135 |зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 33 |зөҢеәҰз§’ = 25.1 | E(жқұзөҢ)еҸҠгҒіW(иҘҝзөҢ) = E | ең°еӣіеӣҪгӮігғјгғү = JP |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|дҪҸеҗүйҖ |12=draft|12=draft}} |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its former ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft|12=draft}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|12=draft|12=draft}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|12=draft|12=draft}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|12=draft|12=draft}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң{{ill|Ushigashira TennЕҚ|ja|зүӣй ӯеӨ©зҺӢ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|1=Gozu TennЕҚ|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft|12=draft}}. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft|12=draft}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of {{ill|MeiЕҚ|ja|жҳҺеҝң|12=draft|12=draft}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|12=draft|12=draft}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ|12=draft|12=draft}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} |жүҖеңЁең° = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|еӨ§йҳӘеәң|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Osaka Sayama City|ja|еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft}} Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft|12=draft}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft|12=draft}}), who is mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft|12=draft}} and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬дёүд»Је®ҹйҢІ|12=draft|12=draft}}'', he was conferred the divine rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the {{ill|JЕҚgan|ja|иІһиҰі|12=draft|12=draft}} era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|12=draft|12=draft}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Osaka Sayama City]] [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ•гӮ„гҒҫгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒІгҒ®гҒҸгҒҫгҒҸгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒҸгҒҶ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sayama Shrine}}
