revision.text:
{{еҮәе…ёгҒ®жҳҺиЁҳ|date=2018-09-09}} {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|29|41.2|N|135|33|25.1|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:ЕҢsakasayama|Osaka Sayama City]] Handa 1-223 |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34 |з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 29 |з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 41.2 | N(еҢ—з·Ҝ)еҸҠгҒіS(еҚ—з·Ҝ) = N |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135 |зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 33 |зөҢеәҰз§’ = 25.1 | E(жқұзөҢ)еҸҠгҒіW(иҘҝзөҢ) = E | ең°еӣіеӣҪгӮігғјгғү = JP |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = [[:en:Sumiyoshi-zukuri|Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Ike|qq=}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, and its former Ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinja YЕҚroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң[[:en:Gozu TennЕҚ|Gozu TennЕҚ]] Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was Ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of [[:en:MeiЕҚ|MeiЕҚ]]. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was Ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|qq=}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisai-no-Kami|qq=}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дәӢд»Јдё»}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮөгғ«гӮҝгғ’гӮі}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irishoku Iri Hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|lt=Seoritsu-Hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж·ӨеҠ зҫҺзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҰгӮ«гғҺгғҹгӮҝгғһ}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:ЕҢsakasayama|Osaka Sayama City]] Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former Ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}), who is mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', he was conferred the divine Rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan]] era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was Ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{translated page|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|version=97797197|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama City|е’ҢжӯҢеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Osakasayama, Osaka]] [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{Commons category|Sayama-jinja}}

{{еҮәе…ёгҒ®жҳҺиЁҳ|date=2018-09-09}} {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|29|41.2|N|135|33|25.1|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:ЕҢsakasayama|Osaka Sayama City]] Handa 1-223 |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34 |з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 29 |з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 41.2 | N(еҢ—з·Ҝ)еҸҠгҒіS(еҚ—з·Ҝ) = N |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135 |зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 33 |зөҢеәҰз§’ = 25.1 | E(жқұзөҢ)еҸҠгҒіW(иҘҝзөҢ) = E | ең°еӣіеӣҪгӮігғјгғү = JP |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = [[:en:Sumiyoshi-zukuri|Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Ike|qq=}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, and its former Ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinja YЕҚroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң[[:en:Gozu TennЕҚ|Gozu TennЕҚ]] Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was Ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of [[:en:MeiЕҚ|MeiЕҚ]]. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was Ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|qq=}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisai-no-Kami|qq=}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дәӢд»Јдё»}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮөгғ«гӮҝгғ’гӮі}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irishoku Iri Hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|lt=Seoritsu-Hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж·ӨеҠ зҫҺзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҰгӮ«гғҺгғҹгӮҝгғһ}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:ЕҢsakasayama|Osaka Sayama City]] Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former Ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}), who is mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', he was conferred the divine Rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan]] era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was Ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{translated page|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|version=97797197|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Villages Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Osakasayama, Osaka]] [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{Commons category|Sayama-jinja}}

{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = [[:en:Sumiyoshi-zukuri|Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Ike|qq=}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, and its former Ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinja YЕҚroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң[[:en:Gozu TennЕҚ|Gozu TennЕҚ]] Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was Ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of [[:en:MeiЕҚ|MeiЕҚ]]. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was Ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|qq=}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:ЕҢsakasayama|Osaka Sayama City]] Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former Ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}), who is mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', he was conferred the divine Rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan]] era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was Ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{translated page|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|version=97797197|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Osakasayama, Osaka]] [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{Commons category|Sayama-jinja}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, and its former Ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinja YЕҚroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң[[:en:Gozu TennЕҚ|Gozu TennЕҚ]] Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was Ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of [[:en:MeiЕҚ|MeiЕҚ]]. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was Ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|qq=}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}вҖқ under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:ЕҢsakasayama|Osaka Sayama City]] Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former Ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}), who is mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', he was conferred the divine Rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan]] era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was Ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{translated page|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|version=97797197|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Osakasayama, Osaka]] [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{Commons category|Sayama-jinja}}

{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = [[:en:Sumiyoshi-zukuri|Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Ike|qq=}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, and its former Ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinja YЕҚroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң[[:en:Gozu TennЕҚ|Gozu TennЕҚ]] Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was Ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of [[:en:MeiЕҚ|MeiЕҚ]]. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was Ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|qq=}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisai-no-Kami|qq=}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дәӢд»Јдё»}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮөгғ«гӮҝгғ’гӮі}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irishoku Iri Hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|lt=Seoritsu-Hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж·ӨеҠ зҫҺзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҰгӮ«гғҺгғҹгӮҝгғһ}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:ЕҢsakasayama|Osaka Sayama City]] Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former Ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}), who is mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', he was conferred the divine Rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan]] era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was Ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{translated page|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|version=97797197|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Osakasayama, Osaka]] [[Category:еӨ§йҳӘзӢӯеұұгҒ® Municipal History]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{Commons category|Sayama-jinja}}

{{еҮәе…ёгҒ®жҳҺиЁҳ|date=2018-09-09}} {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Shrine|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|29|41.2|N|135|33|25.1|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:ЕҢsakasayama|Osaka Sayama City]] Handa 1-223 |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 34 |з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 29 |з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 41.2 | N(еҢ—з·Ҝ)еҸҠгҒіS(еҚ—з·Ҝ) = N |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135 |зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 33 |зөҢеәҰз§’ = 25.1 | E(жқұзөҢ)еҸҠгҒіW(иҘҝзөҢ) = E | ең°еӣіеӣҪгӮігғјгғү = JP |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}} |жң¬ж®ҝ = [[:en:Sumiyoshi-zukuri|Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] |дҫӢзҘӯ = October 10 |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Osaka |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ® = bottom }} '''Sayama Shrine''' (read вҖңSayama JinjaвҖқ) is a Shinto shrine located near the {{ill|Sayama Ike|ja|зӢӯеұұжұ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Ike|qq=}} in [[Osaka Prefecture]]вҖҷs [[Osaka Sayama City]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}}, and its former Ranking was as a {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}}. This article also covers the subsidiary shrine within the precincts, '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine'''. == Enshrined Deities == At Sayama Shrine, the principal deities are: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|еӨ©з…§зҡҮеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu ЕҢmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©з…§еӨ§зҘһ}} and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} as the main enshrined deities, * with {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ancestral deity of the Sayama clan) and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} enshrined as associated deities. Additionally, due to a Meiji period merger of shrines, {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} are also enshrined. According to texts such as ''{{ill|Shinja YЕҚroku|ja|зҘһзӨҫиҰҒйҢІ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinja YЕҚroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸзҘһз§ҒиҖғ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikishin ShikakЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', and the draft version of the ''{{ill|Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|ja|жІіеҶ…еӣҪејҸеҶ…зӨҫзӣ®йҢІзЁҝжң¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi-koku Shikinaisha Mokuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', {{ill|Ominayama no Mikoto|ja|иҮЈзӢӯеұұе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ominayama no Mikoto|qq=}} is regarded as the principal deity. From the Muromachi period through the Edo period, the shrine was also known as the вҖң[[:en:Gozu TennЕҚ|Gozu TennЕҚ]] Shrine.вҖқ == History == Although the exact date of foundation is unknown, tradition holds that the shrine was established before the construction of Sayama Ike, by imperial command from [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' records that together with the nearby Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (currently a subsidiary of this shrine), it was Ranked among the major shrines (Taisha). During the NanbokuchЕҚ period, the area became a battlefield for both Northern and Southern forces, suffering repeated destruction by war. The current shrine buildings are estimated to have been rebuilt in the middle Muromachi period during the 2nd year of [[:en:MeiЕҚ|MeiЕҚ]]. In Meiji 5 (1872), the shrine was Ranked as a Village Shrine. In January of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a вҖңShinsen Heihaku RyЕҚkЕҚ ShinshaвҖқ (a shrine receiving offerings of ritual objects) and on December 26 of the same year, the village shrine Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine (located in the area known as Akashinayama) was merged with it. In Meiji 40, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|е…«йӣІзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|qq=}} (enshrining {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|зҙ зӣһе—ҡе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}}) was merged, and in Meiji 42, {{ill|Sayama Shima Shrine|ja|зӢӯй–“зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Shima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (enshrining {{ill|Inadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|зЁІз”°е§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҜгӮ·гғҠгғҖгғ’гғЎ}} and {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}}) was merged as well. == Subsidiary Shrines == Sayama Shrine has been selected as one of the вҖң{{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}вҖқ (One Hundred Selected Green Spots of Osaka Prefecture).{{Cite web|е’Ңжӣё|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=еӨ§йҳӘгҒҝгҒ©гӮҠгҒ®зҷҫйҒё|publisher=еӨ§йҳӘеәң|accessdate=2016-12-23}} * Minor subsidiary shrine (Massha): Haniyasu Shrine (гҖҗеҹҙе®үе§«еӨ§зҘһгҖ‘ / {{ill|Daisai-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§жӯізҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisai-no-Kami|qq=}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gishi Shrine (гҖҗдјҠејүи«ҫе‘ҪгҖ‘ and {{ill|Sakami|ja|еЎһзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Ebisu Shrine ({{ill|Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»еӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotonushi no ЕҢkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дәӢд»Јдё»}}) вҖ“ formerly a subsidiary of Sayama Shima Shrine (a village shrine) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Gakushu Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|ja|еӨ§еұұзҘҮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢyamatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӘгӮӘгғӨгғһгғ„гғҹ}} and {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|зҢҝз”°еҪҰеӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮөгғ«гӮҝгғ’гӮі}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Tsutsumi Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Irishoku Iri Hiko|ja|е…ҘиүІе…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irishoku Iri Hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) вҖ“ formerly the subsidiary of Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine, part of the Shikinai system. * Minor subsidiary shrine: Ashitama Shrine (enshrining {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} and {{ill|Tennoyanokami|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoyanokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}}) * Minor subsidiary shrine: Mizumoto Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|lt=Seoritsu-Hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|ja|зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зҖ¬з№”жҙҘе§«}} and {{ill|Takagami|ja|й«ҳйҫ—зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takagami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж·ӨеҠ зҫҺзҘһ}}) * Subsidiary shrine (Sessha): Inari Shrine (enshrining {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|е®ҮиіҖеҫЎйӯӮзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҰгӮ«гғҺгғҹгӮҝгғһ}}) вҖ“ originally founded as a village shrine under the name вҖңUka no Mitama JinjaвҖқ in Meiji 35 and merged in Meiji 42 == Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine == {{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = {{ill|Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|ja|зӢӯеұұе ӨзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Tsutsumi Jinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:ЕҢsakasayama|Osaka Sayama City]] Handa 1-223 |зҘӯзҘһ = {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |зӨҫж ј = {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} (Large), Village Shrine |image = [[Image:Sayama jinja haiden.jpg|270px]] |image=Sayama jinja5.jpg }} '''Sayama Tsutsumi Shrine''' is a subsidiary shrine (sessha) located within the precincts of Kago Shrine. It is classified as a Shikinai Taisha, and its former Ranking was that of a village shrine. It enshrines {{ill|Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒиүІе…ҘеҪҰе°Ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inshoku Iri Hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|ja|дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko|2=ja|3=дә”еҚҒз“Ҡж•·е…ҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isono Niohiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Inishikiirihiko|qq=}}), who is mentioned in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and in the account of Emperor Suinin as having вҖңcreated Sayama Ike.вҖқ According to the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', he was conferred the divine Rank of JЕ«go-i Ue on January 27 of the first year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan]] era (859). The ''{{ill|Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|qq=}}'' lists this shrine among the major shrines (Taisha). In Meiji 5 (1872) it was Ranked as a village shrine. On December 26 of Meiji 40 (1907), it was merged into Sayama Shrine as a subsidiary shrine. It is located on the left side when facing the Sayama Shrine Haiden. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[RyЕ«jin Shrine (Osaka Sayama City)|RyЕ«jin Shrine]] вҖ“ A shrine located within Sayama Ike; also known as вҖңSayama Ike Shrine,вҖқ managed by RyЕ«jin Shrine. * [[Omikiyama no Mikoto]] {{translated page|ja|зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ|version=97797197|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Sayama Shrine]] [[ja:зӢӯеұұзҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Osakasayama, Osaka]] [[Category:Municipal History of ЕҢsakasayama]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{Commons category|Sayama-jinja}}
